summary: One-line decomp matching fix in Kirby's ftKb_SpecialNZd.c: ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Coll now calls ftCo_Landing_Enter_Basic after a successful ft_80081D0C air-to-ground collision check instead of calling ftKb_SpecialNSk_80105E8C. The bot report says this made main/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNZd:ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Coll a new 84-byte 100% match.

key_files.change: In ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Coll, replaced ftKb_SpecialNSk_80105E8C(gobj) with ftCo_Landing_Enter_Basic(gobj) after clearing fp->fv.kb.xB4 and fp->mv.kb.specialhi.x4.

what_changed: Changed the landing/collision transition helper used by ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Coll.
Preserved the surrounding condition and state resets: if ft_80081D0C(gobj) succeeds, fp->fv.kb.xB4 and fp->mv.kb.specialhi.x4 are set to 0 before entering the landing action.
The only source diff was one deleted call to ftKb_SpecialNSk_80105E8C(gobj) and one added call to ftCo_Landing_Enter_Basic(gobj).
decomp-dev bot reported matched code increased by +740 bytes overall and specifically listed ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Coll as a new +84 byte 100% match.
smart_moves: The fix targeted the semantic transition point rather than forcing local control-flow or temporary-shape changes: on ground collision, the function uses the shared common landing entry routine.
Using ftCo_Landing_Enter_Basic aligns this Kirby special-air end collision handler with common fighter landing behavior, which was enough to recover the function match.
The patch was minimal and low-risk for decomp purposes: a single call replacement with identical gobj argument and no unrelated formatting or struct changes.
decomp_lessons: When an air special collision end routine fails to match, check whether the original code calls a common landing helper such as ftCo_Landing_Enter_Basic instead of a character-specific special helper.
A wrong callee with the same argument shape can break an otherwise small function; matching may depend on choosing the semantically correct action-transition function.
State-field clears immediately before a transition call are useful context for identifying the intended helper: here fp->fv.kb.xB4 and fp->mv.kb.specialhi.x4 are reset before entering basic landing.
Keep single-callsite decomp fixes narrowly scoped when the surrounding control flow already matches.
naming_conventions: ftKb_ prefix is used for Kirby-specific fighter code.
ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Coll names a Kirby copied/special air neutral end collision handler; the suffix _Coll marks collision handling.
ftCo_ prefix marks common fighter routines; ftCo_Landing_Enter_Basic is the common basic landing action entry.
The file path src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNZd.c groups this function under Kirby character special-N-related code.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Swap candidate callees when the function body differs only at a branch/call target; compare whether the expected transition is common fighter landing versus character-specific special handling.
Use decomp.dev item-level reports to validate tiny fixes: this PR's bot report identified ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Coll moving from 0.00% to 100.00% with +84 bytes.
Do not over-interpret unrelated unit movement in the bot report; the diff only touched ftKb_SpecialNZd.c, while several ef* match/break entries were reported as broader linkage/accounting effects.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
Automated decomp-dev report confirmed a new match for main/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNZd:ftKb_SkSpecialAirNEnd_Coll (+84 bytes, 0.00% to 100.00%).
The same bot report also listed unrelated-looking ef* match and regression movements; evidence for this PR's intended fix is strongest for the Kirby function named in the title and diff.
follow_up_queries: Find other ft*SpecialAir*End_Coll functions that call character-specific helpers after ft_80081D0C where ftCo_Landing_Enter_Basic may be expected.
Compare neighboring Kirby special collision handlers in ftKb_SpecialNZd.c for common landing-transition patterns.
Audit uses of ftKb_SpecialNSk_80105E8C to distinguish valid special-state transitions from places that should use ftCo_Landing_Enter_Basic.
